concepts.lesson: Sound waves are vibrations that travel through a medium, such as air, water, or solids. These vibrations cause the molecules in the medium to oscillate back and forth, creating a series of compressions and rarefactions. The frequency of these vibrations determines the pitch of the sound, while the amplitude determines its loudness.
key_points: Sound waves are caused by vibrations

concepts.lesson: The speed of sound is the rate at which a sound wave propagates through a medium. It depends on the properties of the medium, such as its temperature and pressure. The speed of sound in air at room temperature is approximately 343 meters per second.
key_points: Speed of sound depends on medium properties
Speed of sound in air is approximately 343 m/s

concepts.lesson: Resonance occurs when a system vibrates at its natural frequency, causing it to amplify the vibration. This can lead to destructive effects, such as breaking or shattering of objects. Resonance is often used in musical instruments and other applications where specific frequencies need to be amplified.
key_points: Resonance occurs when a system vibrates at its natural frequency

concepts.lesson: Sound intensity is a measure of the sound pressure level, usually measured in decibels (dB). It depends on the amplitude and frequency of the sound wave. Sound intensity can be calculated using the formula: I = P / A, where I is the intensity, P is the power, and A is the area.
key_points: Sound intensity measures sound pressure level
Can be calculated using the formula: I = P / A

concepts.lesson: The Doppler effect is a phenomenon where the frequency of a sound wave changes when its source is moving relative to an observer. This causes the pitch of the sound to change, becoming higher or lower depending on the direction and speed of the motion.
key_points: Doppler effect occurs when source is moving
Depends on direction and speed of motion
